name: ExtensionServiceRequest_PatientInstruction
title: R5: Patient or consumer-oriented instructions
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` 0..* `BackboneElement`
*  R4B: `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` 0..1 `string`
*  R4: `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction.instruction[x]` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction.instruction[x]` has a context of ServiceRequest based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ServiceRequest`.
Element `ServiceRequest.patientInstruction.instruction[x]` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: Instructions in terms that are understood by the patient or consumer.
